{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.2": 5, "RX.1": 3, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 5},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs for each issue identified.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for two claims: the cost arithmetic and the resolution of the portability/labeling tension in Section 5.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed dimensions without any drift.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions are introduced, and the agent identifies a potential harmful pattern in the original (the non sequitur from AUC to accuracy gap).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, providing clear traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.2": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.8, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.9},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs for each issue identified.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for two claims: the cost arithmetic and the resolution of the portability/labeling tension in Section 5.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed dimensions without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions are introduced, and the agent identifies a potential harmful pattern in the original (the non sequitur from AUC to accuracy gap).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, providing clear traceability.
